exercises.name: Hyperextension Roman Chair Back Extension
exercises.description:
1) Lie face down on a hyperextension bench with your body in a straight line. Arms in a cross position in your chest.

2) Bend forward at the waist as far as you can. Make sure your back is flat at ALL times.

3) Then raise your body back up until your body is in a straight line again.

exercises.name: Improve Your Posture: Doorway Chest Stretch
exercises.description: Improve Your Posture: Doorway Chest Stretch

exercises.name: Inchworm Walk
exercises.description:
1) Get into a plank position on your hands and toes, with arms straight, and core engaged. 

2) With very small steps from your ankles and hips, while keeping your knees straight, move your lower body forward. Try to keep your tailbone tipped up to the ceiling as best you can.  

3) Once you've gone as far as you can without bending your knees, set your feet in place. Then start walking your hands out in little steps until you've returned to a plank position. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Incline Cable Overhead Tricep Extension Face Down
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on an incline bench facing away from a cable system, holding a rope at head height behind your head with your elbows bent.

2.Straighten your arms overhead, extending your elbows fully.

3.Bend at the elbows, returning the cable back to starting position.

exercises.name: Incline Hex Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on an incline bench holding hex dumbbells with palms facing each other.
 
Lower the weights to your chest, then press them back up, maintaining the neutral grip to engage more of the chest muscles.

exercises.name: Incline Low to High Plank
exercises.description:
Start in an incline plank with hands on a raised surface. 

Lower one elbow to the surface, then the other. Return to hands. Alternate lead arms.

exercises.name: Incline narrow push ups on bench
Incline Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Using an object to create an incline with your body, support your body on your toes, legs and arms straight.

2.Lower your chest, elbows bent.

3.Push up to a straight arm, returning to starting position.

4.Return.

exercises.name: Incline Push Up with Renegade Row
exercises.description:
Place two dumbbells on a bench and get into a push-up position with your hands gripping the dumbbells and your body in a straight line. 

Perform a push-up, then row one dumbbell toward your ribs while keeping your core tight and body stable. Lower it back down, repeat the push-up, and alternate sides for each row.

exercises.name: Incline Push-Up to Shoulder Tap
exercises.description:
Place your hands on a bench or elevated platform and get into a plank position with your body in a straight line. 

Perform a push-up, then lift one hand to tap the opposite shoulder while keeping your hips as stable as possible. 

Lower your hand back down and repeat on the other side, alternating shoulder taps after each push-up.

exercises.name: Inverted Row
exercises.description:
1) Set the bar at your hip level and position yourself underneath the bar, hanging from it with a shoulder width grip and your heels on the floor. 

2) Begin by pulling yourself upwards until your chest touches the bar, pause, and lower yourself to the starting position.

exercises.name: Is Ys and Ts
exercises.description:
1) For this corrective exercise, it’s important to keep your shoulder blades back and down. It’s called scapular setting. 

2) The “I” position is the most challenging, it’s straight up overhead. 

3) The “Y” is out at 45 degrees, again put your shoulder blades down. 

4) “T” is at 90 degrees shoulder blades down. 

5) To do this exercise, you lie down forward. 

6) Set your shoulder blades and lift your arms above the ground. 

7) Pick the one that’s suited for you and your capacity to hold your shoulder blades in place.
